description_long: This object is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, faceted golden horn speaker mounted on a sturdy, dark brown wooden base. The horn is polygonal and flares outward, giving it a classic, old-fashioned appearance. The base is rectangular and layered, with a visible turntable platter on top, which is black with a small, colored label in the center. A tonearm extends from the right side of the base, positioned over the platter, and appears to be made of a dark material. The overall design is geometric and stylized, with sharp edges and blocky forms. No dust cover or visible control knobs are present, emphasizing the retro aesthetic.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large golden horn, dark wooden base, and a visible black turntable platter with a colored label.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large golden horn, black platter with colored label, and the tonearm extending from the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the horn and the rectangular wooden base, with the tonearm visible on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the horn and the base, with the platter and label still visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile and the tonearm's full length, with the base's layered structure.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black with a small, square colored label in the center. It occupies a prominent position on the top surface of the base and appears to be made of plastic or metal. The platter is smooth, with no visible grooves or patterns, and contrasts with the wooden base.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, extending from the right side of the base toward the platter. It is straight, medium in length, and appears to be made of a dark material, likely metal or plastic. The tonearm is positioned over the platter, suggesting it is ready to play a record.
